summary: Frontend e2e testing of the new per-video isolated tools pages. All 50 assertions passed at 420px mobile viewport. Verified: (1) videos listing shows lean cards with no inline 'FERRAMENTAS CITADAS' section; (2) cards with tools/pdf show 'Acessar Ferramentas' that navigates to /ferramentas/<slug>; (3) card without tools (google-storage) has NO access-tools button; (4) STRICT isolation confirmed on /ferramentas/07072026v1 (exactly 5 tools: NetMovies, freeCodeCamp, Diolinux, Guia Anônima, Asimov Academy; no Photopea, PimEyes, ToolFK, etc.); (5) /ferramentas/05072026v1 shows exactly the 6 expected tools and no cross-contamination; (6) /ferramentas/11072026v1 shows ONLY ferramenta-pdf and no other tools; (7) all tool buttons open in new tab (target=_blank); (8) slug route /07072026v1 opens VideoSingle with correct title and share link containing the slug; (9) legacy /v/policia still works; (10) /ferramentas without slug redirects to /videos; (11) unknown slug shows tools-not-found.
critical_code_review_comments: Ferramentas.jsx uses tool.name inside data-testid via lowercase+replace whitespace and strips dots — collisions could occur if two videos happened to share the same tool name (e.g. TinyWow appears in both 5-sites-parecem-ilegais and 5-sites-facilitar-vida), but since each page is isolated by slug this is not an issue in practice.

Videos.jsx catch-all route /:slug for VideoSingle correctly comes AFTER /apps, /videos, /ferramentas etc. Note: any future top-level route name that matches an unrelated word could conflict — keep new top-level routes above the catch-all.
'Acessar Ferramentas' is only rendered when hasResources; visually consistent. No regressions found.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Static React site, no backend. Base URL: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env. Key routes tested: /videos (listing with tabs investigacao/seguranca/entretenimento/truques/renda), /ferramentas/<slug> (per-video isolated tools), /:slug and /v/:id (single video), /ferramentas (redirects to /videos). data-testids used: videos-page, tab-<catid>, video-<id>, video-date-<id>, watch-<id>, access-tools-<id>, tools-page, tools-page-title, tools-list, tools-watch-video, ferramenta-<toolname-lowercased-dashed>, ferramenta-pdf, tools-not-found, video-single-page, share-block, copy-share-link. Isolation verified across 3 tools pages.
rca of the issue: No issues. The bug reported (global /ferramentas mixing all tools) has been correctly fixed: /ferramentas now redirects to /videos, and per-video tools live at /ferramentas/:slug. VideoCard.jsx builds the link as `/ferramentas/${video.slug || video.id}` and Ferramentas.jsx uses findVideo(slug) to resolve exactly one video, rendering only that video's tools + pdf.
